Hi Ben, It’s great that you and Sam 1
 (come) to visit me next weekend. It
2
 (be) fantastic to see you. There’s a new cafe in town called Green’s, so I
3
 (try) to book a table there for lunch. Let me know when you
4
 (leave), and I 5
 (make) sure that everything is ready for you. We
6
 (have) so much fun, I can’t wait! See you on Saturday! Zak

2. will be
3. will try
4. are leaving
5. will make
6. are going to have

Read the dialogue and choose the correct answer.
1 Girl Where is drama club tonight?
Boy I think it’s on the in the school hall.
Girl OK. I’ll see you there.
A ☐ flute
B ☐ stage
C ☐ choir
2 Boy Oh no! There’s something wrong with my bike!
Girl I don’t think it’s serious. Pass me those and I’ll try to repair it.
Boy Thanks!
A ☐ tools
B ☐ robotics
C ☐ costumes
3 Girl Why didn’t you tell me about the party last night?
Boy I texted you! Why don’t you ever ?
Girl I was really busy last night!
A ☐ check your messages
B ☐ read a book
C ☐ listen to music
4 Boy We need some local people to help to tidy the on Saturday. Can you come?
Girl Sure. I’m not busy.
A ☐ orchestra
B ☐ community garden
C ☐ nature photography
5 Woman I heard that you had an accident. What happened?
Man I was on my phone and I walked into a door!
Woman Oh, no!
A ☐ going to the gym
B ☐ scrolling through social media
C ☐ making a cake
6 Girl How often do you play games on the internet?
Boy Quite often. But I enjoy , too.
Girl Me too!
A ☐ board games
B ☐ multi-player online games
C ☐ costumes

1. B ☑ stage
2. A ☑ tools
3. A ☑ check your messages
4. B ☑ community garden
5. B ☑ scrolling through social media
6. A ☑ board games

F Reading: Understanding new words
Read the article about a famous teacher and her pupil and choose the best option to answer
the questions.
A famous teacher and her pupil
Anne Sullivan is one of the world’s most famous teachers. During her life, she only had one
student. This was American author and disability rights campaigner Helen Keller, who later in her
life fought for people like herself to have the same educational and employment opportunities as
everyone else. When the two first met in 1887, they could not communicate with each other. At
that time, Helen was blind, deaf and mute. She lost her sight and hearing after she became ill at
the age of 19 months, which meant that she didn’t learn to speak as a child.
Anne was 20 when she started working for the Kellers. Helen was seven, and her parents had no
control over her. After the first week, Anne took Helen away from the main house to live with her
in her cottage.
Anne was the perfect teacher because she understood some of Helen’s problems. When Anne
was five, she was ill. She contracted an infection that damaged her sight. As a teenager, she
attended the Perkins School for the Blind. Here, she learned to use the manual alphabet, but it
was a difficult time for her as she endured several unsuccessful operations to improve her
eyesight while she was at the school.
At first, Helen did not understand finger spelling. Then one day, she was washing her hands. Anne
took one hand and spelled W-A-T-E-R on it. From that moment on, they could communicate.
Helen went on to learn nearly 600 words in only a few months. She also became familiar with
some multiplication tables and the Braille writing system, which uses raised dots you read with
your fingers. Later, Helen learned to lip-read by placing her fingers on the lips of the speaker.
Eventually, Anne persuaded Helen’s parents to send Helen to the Perkins School for the Blind.
Anne went with her. She continued to tutor Helen although she also had classes at school. Helen
made excellent progress and later got a place at Radcliffe College. With Anne’s help, Helen
ultimately became the first deaf and blind person to get a degree, which was the result of her
hard work and her teacher’s energy and time.
Anne stayed with Helen even after she graduated. Together they gave lectures about Helen’s
achievements. Thanks to her amazing teacher, Helen Keller was able to lead a full and happy life
despite the problems she had.
1 The word campaigner in the article describes someone who .
A ☐ is a bad example of something
B ☐ tries to bring political or social change peacefully
C ☐ uses violent protest to achieve their aims
Life Vision Poland Pre-intermediate • Unit Test 5 B
IMAGES NOT TO BE USED SEPARATELY. FOR CUSTOMER USE ONLY © Oxford University Press
4
2 The words in the article that tell you that mute means unable to talk are .
A ☐ she became ill at the age of 19 months
B ☐ she lost her sight and hearing
C ☐ she didn’t learn to speak as a child
3 Another way of saying contracted an infection is .
A ☐ had a bad accident
B ☐ had a serious illness
C ☐ had a problem with her eyes
4 The words in the article that explain the manual alphabet are .
A ☐ took one hand and spelled W-A-T-E-R on it
B ☐ raised dots you can read with your fingers
C ☐ placing her fingers on the lips of the speaker
5 The phrase endured several operations means .
A ☐ had several operations
B ☐ requested several operations
C ☐ enjoyed several operations
6 The writer uses the word ultimately to express the idea that .
A ☐ it was disappointing that Helen got a degree
B ☐ it was very easy for Helen to get a degree
C ☐ it was a hard process for Helen to get a degree

1. B ☑ tries to bring political or social change peacefully
2. C ☑ she didn’t learn to speak as a child
3. B ☑ had a serious illness
4. A ☑ took one hand and spelled W-A-T-E-R on it
5. A ☑ had several operations
6. C ☑ it was a hard process for Helen to get a degree

H Speaking: Recalling set phrases
Choose the best sentences to complete the dialogue. There are two sentences you don't
need.
Speaker 1 Have you been to the new snack bar yet?
Speaker 2 No, I haven’t. 1

Speaker 1 I like that idea. When shall we go?
Speaker 2 2

Speaker 1 I’m not sure about that. We’ve got a lot of homework tonight.
Speaker 2 3

Speaker 1 I’d rather not. I have my Italian class on Thursdays.
Speaker 2 4

Speaker 1 Let’s do that. We haven’t got school the next day. Shall we meet there at around
6.30?
Speaker 2 5
 It closes at 7.00.
Speaker 1 Let’s go straight after school then. We could ask Isabel and Jenny to come too.
Speaker 2 6
 I’m sure they’d like to.
A Well, then we could go on Friday.
B How about stopping on the way home from school this afternoon?
C Let’s see if anyone else wants to come.
D Yes, why not?
E Why don’t we try it?
F I don’t think that’s a great idea.
G Let’s meet at your house.
H Do you fancy going tomorrow then?

1. E Why don’t we try it?
2. H Do you fancy going tomorrow then?
3. A Well, then we could go on Friday.
4. F I don’t think that’s a great idea.
5. D Yes, why not?
6. C Let’s see if anyone else wants to come.

I Writing: Connecting ideas
Read the text and choose the correct answer.
Many students want exams to stop. They say classwork is enough. 1
 , many people think
exams are the only way to understand a student's performance.
There are pros and cons to exams. 2
 , they can cause students a lot of stress. 3
 ,
passing an exam is a good way of showing how well you know a subject.
Those against exams say that 4
 some students work hard, they forget things under
stress. Some students feel less stressed, 5
 it could mean they don't care about their
results.
Exams can be frightening.6
 , they don't need to be.
1 A ☐ Although B ☐ However C ☐ On the one hand
2 A ☐ But B ☐ On the one hand C ☐ However
3 A ☐ Although B ☐ On the other hand C ☐ On the one hand
4 A ☐ however B ☐ although C ☐ but
5 A ☐ on the one hand B ☐ but C ☐ however
6 A ☐ On the one hand B ☐ However C ☐ On the other hand

1. B ☑ However
2. B ☑ On the one hand
3. B ☑ On the other hand
4. B ☑ although
5. C ☑ however
6. C ☑ On the other hand

H Speaking: Recalling set phrases
Choose the best sentences to complete the dialogue. There are two sentences you don't
need.
Speaker 1 Have you got a present for Zak yet?
Speaker 2 No, I haven’t. 1

Speaker 1 That sounds like a great idea. We’ll have twice as much money, so we can get him
something nice.
Speaker 2 2

Speaker 1 I’d rather not. I’ve got basketball practice tonight, so we won’t have much time.
Speaker 2 3

Speaker 1 OK, I’m free. Let’s do that.
Speaker 2 4
 I haven’t been there yet.
Speaker 1 Yes, why not? I think there’s a sports shop there. We could get Zak some trainers.
Speaker 2 5
 We don’t know his size.
Speaker 1 Right. Why don’t we get him a computer game, then?
Speaker 2 6
 That’s his favourite hobby!
A How about going tomorrow, then?
B Let’s think about what we could get him.
C I like that idea.
D Do you fancy getting him something together?
E We could get the bus to the new shopping centre in the morning.
F Why don’t we get him a smartwatch?
G I’m not sure about that.
H Shall we go shopping this afternoon?

1. D Do you fancy getting him something together?
2. A How about going tomorrow, then?
3. E We could get the bus to the new shopping centre in the morning.
4. C I like that idea.
5. G I’m not sure about that.
6. F Why don’t we get him a smartwatch?

D
Do you fancy getting him something together? is
correct because something refers back to a present in
Speaker 1’s question.
© Oxford University Press Life Vision Poland Pre-intermediate • Answer key 4
2 H
Shall we go shopping this afternoon? is correct because
Speaker 1’s response I’ve got basketball practice
tonight, so we won’t have much time refers to Speaker
2's suggestion of this afternoon.
3 A
How about going tomorrow, then? is correct because
Speaker 2 suggests a different day, and Speaker 1
accepts the suggestion in the following response.
4 E
We could get the bus to the new shopping centre in the
morning is correct because Speaker 2 suggests a place
to go shopping, and Speaker 1 accepts the suggestion in
the following response.
5 G
I’m not sure about that is correct because Speaker 2
rejects Speaker 1’s suggestion to get trainers because
We don’t know his size.
6 C
I like that idea is correct because Speaker 2 accepts
Speaker 1’s suggestion to get a computer game because
That’s his favourite hobby!

Thank you for pointing out the correct allocations and flow of the dialogue based on the context provided. Here's a recap of the correct sequence and rationale for each choice based on your explanation:
 
1. D Do you fancy getting him something together? - This option naturally follows the revelation that no present has been bought yet, suggesting a joint effort to purchase a gift, which aligns well with the concept of pooling resources for a better gift.

2. H Shall we go shopping this afternoon? - Correctly identified as the initial suggestion for timing, which prompts a logistical issue due to basketball practice, indicating the necessity for an alternative time.

3. A How about going tomorrow, then? - This becomes the viable alternative when the immediate afternoon plan doesn't fit due to prior commitments, and it's positively received as a solution.

4. E We could get the bus to the new shopping centre in the morning - This suggestion specifies the logistics of visiting a shopping center the next day, matching with the agreed-upon timing and is met with approval.

5. G I’m not sure about that - Given as a response to a specific gift suggestion, which is met with uncertainty due to incomplete information about preferences or sizes, prompting a need to rethink the gift choice.

6. C I like that idea - This final acceptance matches the alternative suggestion that aligns with Zak's interests, concluding the dialogue on a positive note of agreement. 

This breakdown confirms the flow of decision-making from realizing a gift is needed, coordinating a time to shop, navigating logistical challenges, to finally agreeing on a suitable gift idea.

B Grammar: Zero and first conditional
Read the sentence and choose the correct answer.
1 If we our trainers, we can’t play basketball.
A ☐ aren’t bringing B ☐ won’t bring C ☐ don’t bring
2 If you , you won’t get a good mark.
A ☐ don’t study B ☐ won’t study C ☐ will study
3 If Beth leaves now, in time.
A ☐ she arrives B ☐ she’ll arrive C ☐ she arrived
4 If it snows, our school sometimes .
A ☐ will close B ☐ closes C ☐ closed
5 If Tom goes to the party, he his essay.
A ☐ didn’t finish B ☐ won’t finish C ☐ doesn’t finish
6 When you sit at a desk all day, you enough exercise.
A ☐ don’t get B ☐ won’t get C ☐ didn't get

1. C ☑ don’t bring
2. A ☑ don’t study
3. B ☑ she’ll arrive
4. B ☑ closes
5. B ☑ won’t finish
6. A ☑ don’t get

C Vocabulary: Understanding topic vocabulary
Choose the correct words from the box to complete the sentences. There are two words you
do not need to use.
qualification folder grade ruler assignment marker term canteen
1 Does this school finish in July or August?
2 Aisha got a better than me in maths this year.
3 I forgot to put my homework in my this morning.
4 You need to have a in French for that job.
5 Your English this week is to write a review of a book.
6 The teacher used a red to write the words on the board.

1. term
2. grade
3. folder
4. qualification
5. assignment
6. marker
